# Feature Specification: Specs Index Generator

**Feature Branch**: `002-specs-index-generator`
**Created**: 2026-05-08
**Status**: Draft (auto-generated by /speckit.specify; ready for /speckit.clarify)
**Input**: User description: "Generate a specs/INDEX.md that lists every spec folder, its status (Draft / Clarified / Planned / Tasks-ready / Implementing / Shipped), the feature branch, and the closing PR number. The index is regenerated by a small Node script that scans specs/*/ and reads each spec.md frontmatter or top-of-file metadata. Status is inferred from which artefacts exist in the folder. The script runs from a pre-commit hook and from a daily n8n cron. Out of scope: a UI — operators read INDEX.md directly."

> Test feature for the spec-kit tracer-bullet (PR B in the activation gaps plan). The point is to exercise the canonical /speckit.specify → /speckit.clarify → /speckit.plan → /speckit.tasks → /speckit.analyze loop end-to-end against a real feature, watching for script breakage and hook firings. The actual generator implementation is deferred; this PR ships only the spec-kit artefacts.

## Clarifications

### Session 2026-05-08

- Q: What language should the generator script be written in? → A: TypeScript via `npx tsx scripts/generate-specs-index.ts` (Option A). Matches FURQAN's stack (TS 5 + Next.js 16), zero new toolchain (tsx already in devDeps or trivially added), easy to unit-test alongside the existing vitest setup, and any future contributor already knows TypeScript from the rest of the codebase.
- Q: Does FURQAN already have a pre-commit hook framework? → A: No — install husky + lint-staged in this PR (Option A). FURQAN has no `.husky/` directory or `prepare` script today. At 50k user scale the team and agent-fleet will grow; standard tooling is the cheapest path. Pre-commit hook lives at `.husky/pre-commit` and runs `npx tsx scripts/generate-specs-index.ts` only when staged files match `specs/**/*.md`.
- Q: What identity authors the cron-driven INDEX.md drift-correction commits? → A: Use the existing `drdeebtech@gmail.com` git identity (per CLAUDE.md "Git Identity Rule"; Vercel deploy gate checks every commit author). Commit subject MUST be prefixed with `[index-bot]` so commits are filterable in `git log` via `git log --grep='\[index-bot\]'`. Avoids the operational cost of provisioning + Vercel-approving a separate bot account; the message-prefix gives the filterability that matters without identity sprawl. Promote to a dedicated bot identity if/when the team grows past one human.
- Q: Where does the generator read the feature branch name from — spec.md frontmatter, live `gh` lookup, or both? → A: Spec.md frontmatter (or top-of-file `**Feature Branch**: \`xxx\`` metadata, matching the existing template) is the canonical source. `gh pr list --head <branch>` is used only for PR state (open / merged / closed-not-merged). PR-state lookups are cached for the duration of one regen run. Decision rationale at 50k scale: ≤30 in-flight specs × 1 PR-API call per regen = ≤30 calls cacheable; reading frontmatter is zero-network-cost; live branch lookup would multiply API cost without benefit (the spec.md author writes the branch name explicitly via /speckit.specify).
- Q: How does the index treat a feature whose most-recent PR is closed-but-not-merged (abandoned)? → A: Add a new status `Abandoned` to FR-003's lifecycle vocabulary. Abandoned specs appear in a separate "Abandoned (last 90 days)" section in INDEX.md, sorted by close date. After 90 days the row auto-disappears from the index, though the spec folder itself stays on disk for archaeology. Decision rationale at 50k scale: without a recency cap, INDEX.md becomes a graveyard that defeats SC-001's "find a spec in 10 seconds" outcome; 90 days is forgiving but bounded.

## User Scenarios & Testing *(mandatory)*

### User Story 1 — Operator finds the right spec in seconds (Priority: P1)

An operator (or AI agent) opens `specs/INDEX.md` and within 10 seconds sees: every feature spec that exists in the repo, its current state in the spec-kit lifecycle, the feature branch name, and the merged-or-open PR number. No need to `ls specs/` and read individual spec files to figure out which features are draft vs. clarified vs. shipped.

**Why this priority**: Without the index, finding "what specs exist and where they are in the workflow" requires running 3-5 grep/find commands per investigation. At 10+ specs in flight, this becomes the slowest part of any "what's the state of feature X" question. P1 because every other story depends on the index existing.

**Independent Test**: Open `specs/INDEX.md` on a repo with 3 specs at different lifecycle stages (e.g., murajaah-scheduler at "Clarified+Planned", a fresh test at "Draft", a shipped one at "Shipped"). Verify the page lists all three with the correct status badge, branch name, and (if present) PR number.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** specs/ has 3 feature directories at different lifecycle stages, **When** an operator opens `specs/INDEX.md`, **Then** they see one row per feature with status, branch, and PR columns populated.
2. **Given** the operator clicks/links from a row to its `spec.md`, **When** they navigate, **Then** the row's path links resolve correctly.
3. **Given** a feature has no `plan.md` yet, **When** the index is generated, **Then** that feature shows status "Draft" or "Clarified" (whichever applies), not "Planned".

---

### User Story 2 — INDEX.md stays current without manual updates (Priority: P2)

When a contributor commits changes to any `specs/*/spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, or contracts file, a pre-commit hook regenerates `INDEX.md` so the next reader sees current state.

**Why this priority**: Manual upkeep of an index file is exactly the failure mode that causes today's stale-branch problem. P2 because the index is useful even with manual regen (Story 1 ships value), but auto-regen is what makes it durable past the first month.

**Independent Test**: From a clean working tree, edit any `specs/<feature>/spec.md`. Run `git commit`. Verify `INDEX.md` was updated in the same commit (or in a follow-on commit by the hook).

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** a contributor edits `specs/<feature>/plan.md`, **When** they commit, **Then** `INDEX.md` reflects the feature's new "Planned" status without manual intervention.
2. **Given** a feature folder is renamed (e.g., during the speckit feature-renaming workflow), **When** the rename commits, **Then** INDEX.md picks up the new path on next run.

---

### User Story 3 — Daily cron catches drift (Priority: P3)

If a contributor bypasses the pre-commit hook (e.g., `--no-verify`) or merges via the GitHub UI without re-running the hook, a daily n8n cron regenerates `INDEX.md` and commits any drift it finds. The cron commit author is a bot identity so the drift-correction commit is visible in `git log` and doesn't pretend to be a human.

**Why this priority**: P3 because the pre-commit hook covers 95% of cases; the cron is belt-and-suspenders for edge cases. The feature is useful without the cron, but lower-confidence over time.

**Independent Test**: Manually edit `specs/<feature>/plan.md` while bypassing the pre-commit hook. Run the cron job manually. Verify the resulting `INDEX.md` reflects the change.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** a contributor pushes a commit that didn't re-run the pre-commit hook, **When** the daily cron runs, **Then** it detects the drift, regenerates INDEX.md, and either commits the fix (if changes are present) or no-ops (if INDEX.md is current).

---

### Edge Cases

- **`specs/` is empty.** INDEX.md generates with a "No specs yet" placeholder; doesn't error.
- **A spec folder exists but has no `spec.md`.** That folder is reported with status `Malformed` and a warning emitted to `automation_logs` (consistent with FURQAN's existing warning pattern).
- **A feature has both an open PR and a merged PR (because someone opened a follow-up).** Status is "Implementing" if the most recent PR is open; "Shipped" only when the most recent PR for that feature is merged. The index links to the most recent PR.
- **A feature's branch was deleted post-merge** (per the Branch Hygiene Rule). INDEX.md status falls back to "Shipped" using the closing PR's metadata; no warning.
- **Two specs reference the same feature branch name.** Index reports both rows with a warning ("branch X is referenced by 2 specs"); operator decides which is canonical.

## Requirements *(mandatory)*

### Functional Requirements

- **FR-001**: System MUST scan all `specs/<feature-dir>/` folders at the repo root and produce a markdown table at `specs/INDEX.md` with one row per feature.
- **FR-002**: Each row MUST include the feature directory name, the inferred lifecycle status, the feature branch name (read from spec.md frontmatter or top-of-file metadata), and the most recent PR number (open or merged).
- **FR-003**: Lifecycle status MUST be inferred from which artefacts exist in the folder, in this exact precedence: most-recent PR is merged → `Shipped`; most-recent PR is open → `Implementing`; most-recent PR is closed-but-not-merged → `Abandoned`; no PR yet AND `tasks.md` exists → `Tasks-ready`; `plan.md` exists → `Planned`; spec.md contains a `## Clarifications` section with at least one Q→A bullet → `Clarified`; otherwise → `Draft`. `Abandoned` rows are confined to a separate "Abandoned (last 90 days)" section in INDEX.md; rows older than 90 days auto-disappear from the index (folder stays on disk).
- **FR-004**: A pre-commit hook (managed by `husky` + `lint-staged`, installed in this PR) MUST regenerate INDEX.md when any file under `specs/**/*.md` is staged in the commit. Hook lives at `.husky/pre-commit`; it shells to `npx tsx scripts/generate-specs-index.ts` only when the staged file pattern matches. The hook is non-blocking — if regeneration fails, the hook logs the error but does NOT block the commit (per Constitution Principle II — Loud Failures: surface but don't block trivial-path work). Stages the regenerated INDEX.md alongside the commit if changed.
- **FR-005**: A nightly cron at **03:00 UTC** (n8n on the Mac mini per CLAUDE.md cron policy) MUST regenerate INDEX.md and commit any drift. Author of the cron commit is the existing `drdeebtech@gmail.com` git identity (per Git Identity Rule); commit subject MUST be prefixed with `[index-bot]` so the commit is filterable via `git log --grep='\[index-bot\]'`. If no drift, the cron is a no-op (no empty commit).
- **FR-006**: Feature branch name MUST be read from spec.md's `**Feature Branch**: \`<name>\`` line (already in the template). PR-state lookups (`gh pr list --head <branch>`) MUST cache results for the duration of one regen run. At 50k user scale, total in-flight specs are bounded (estimated ≤30), so total API call cost per regen is ≤30 cached lookups. The script MUST NOT do live branch-existence lookup (frontmatter is canonical) and MUST NOT call the GitHub API per-row.
- **FR-007**: Edge-case handling: malformed feature folders (missing spec.md) MUST appear in the index with status `Malformed` and a warning emitted to `automation_logs.status='failed'` per the existing automation pattern.
- **FR-008**: INDEX.md generation MUST be idempotent — running it twice without changes produces no diff.
- **FR-009**: The generator MUST be implemented as a TypeScript module run via `npx tsx scripts/generate-specs-index.ts`. The script lives at `scripts/generate-specs-index.ts`; tests live at `scripts/__tests__/generate-specs-index.test.ts` and run under the existing vitest setup. Any spec-kit-related tooling Node entrypoint follows the same convention going forward.
- **FR-010**: When the cron's `gh` CLI invocation fails (transient auth, GitHub API outage, rate limit), the wrapper script MUST exit non-zero so n8n's existing self-healing pattern (Telegram alert + automatic retry on next tick) handles recovery. The script MUST NOT silently fall back to "no PR data" rows — that would silently degrade INDEX.md correctness. Subsequent retries on n8n's standard cadence catch up the next morning. No new credential rotation pattern is required; existing `gh` auth on the Mac mini is the single source of truth.

### Key Entities

- **Spec folder** — a directory under `specs/` with the convention `<NNN>-<short-name>` (per spec-kit init-options branch_numbering=sequential). Contains at minimum `spec.md`; optionally `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `research.md`, `data-model.md`, `quickstart.md`, `contracts/`.
- **Lifecycle status** — one of `Draft | Clarified | Planned | Tasks-ready | Implementing | Shipped | Abandoned | Malformed`. Derived by file-existence inspection plus GitHub PR state lookup. `Abandoned` is a recency-bounded transient state (90-day window per FR-003).
- **INDEX.md** — the generated markdown file at `specs/INDEX.md`. One header row + one row per spec folder, sorted by NNN prefix ascending.

## Success Criteria *(mandatory)*

### Measurable Outcomes

- **SC-001**: An operator finds the lifecycle status of any spec in under 10 seconds by reading INDEX.md (vs. 30–60 seconds today via `ls + cat` scrolling).
- **SC-002**: INDEX.md drift between commits (the time it's stale before the next regen) is under 1 minute on commit-path; under 24 hours on the cron-only path.
- **SC-003**: 100% of spec folders are represented in INDEX.md (no silent omission). A folder missing from the index is a regression.
- **SC-004**: Zero stale rows after merge — when a PR closes, INDEX.md reflects "Shipped" within the next cron cycle (≤24h).

## Assumptions

- This PR installs the pre-commit hook framework (husky + lint-staged) — FURQAN has none today. Future spec-kit-related hooks ride on the same framework.
- The n8n on the Mac mini can authenticate to GitHub via an existing CRON_SECRET pattern. No new credential infrastructure required.
- Spec folders follow the `<NNN>-<short-name>` convention (per spec-kit init-options.json `branch_numbering: sequential`). Folders that don't match are ignored (or reported as malformed if they contain spec.md anyway).
- Operators read INDEX.md in plain text or rendered markdown — no HTML/UI needed (per "out of scope" in the description).

## Out of scope

- A web UI to view the index (per description). Operators read INDEX.md directly.
- Filtering/searching/sorting controls beyond the static markdown table.
- Cross-repo aggregation (this is single-repo only).
- Spec content quality checks (those are `/speckit.analyze`'s job).
- Branch lifecycle automation beyond reading the current state (deletion / archival is the Branch Hygiene Rule's job, not this).
- Notifying contributors when their spec status changes — that's a Communication-domain concern, deferred.

## Cross-references

- `.specify/memory/constitution.md` — checked on /speckit.plan run (Constitution Gate).
- `CLAUDE.md` "Spec-Kit Workflow" — defines the lifecycle stages this index reports on.
- `CLAUDE.md` "Branch Hygiene Rule" — assumes branch-deletion is handled there; INDEX.md reports current state, doesn't enforce hygiene.
- `CLAUDE.md` cron policy: "Cron jobs go on n8n, not Vercel" — the daily cron lives on the Mac mini per FR-005.
